title: lapsana communis subsp. pinnatisecta
intro: Lapsana communis subsp. pinnatisecta, also known as prickly lettuce or wild lettuce, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Lapsana communis subsp. pinnatisecta include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve sleep quality, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its leaves can also be eaten raw or cooked as a leafy green vegetable.
